problem type: mip problem info: - seaport unloads shipping off cars, holds them its yard transport system, then loads them onto - \var{demand_{t}} containers ships every month, this demand must be fulfilled. - unloading cost \var{unload_cost_{t}} container indicates how it unload single container rail - unloading capacity \var{unload_capacity_{t}} specifies maximum number containers can be unloaded month. - each container kept yard subsequent month, there is holding cost $\var{holding_cost}. - due space yard can store maximum containers end any - initially, start first month, yard has \var{init_container} containers. - yard should not contain any after end last month. - additionally, loading containers onto ships necessitates rental cranes. - maximum \var{num_cranes} cranes can be rented each month, each having capacity load \var{crane_capacity} containers. - it costs \var{crane_cost} each crane. input format: { "demands": [demand_{t} t <num>, ..., t], "unload_costs": [unload_cost_{t} t <num>, ..., t], "unload_capacity": [unload_capacity_{t} t <num>, t], "holding_cost": holding_cost, max_container, "init_container": init_container, "num_cranes": num_cranes, "crane_capacity": "crane_cost": crane_cost, } objective: determine strategy minimize costs unloading, crane rentals period. output info: - \var{amount_{t}} indicates number containers unloaded each month. - \var{crane_{t}} specifies number cranes each month. - \var{total_cost} shows total cost company over period. format: { "containers_unloaded": [amount_{t} t <num>, ..., t], "cranes_rented": [crane_{t} t <num>, ..., t], "total_cost": total_cost
